rationale.root_cause: They correctly pointed to line 11 and the unsafe strcpy of argv into buf, but did not name the sanitizer class/global_buffer_overflow as required. The term used was only 'buffer overflow', not 'global_buffer_overflow'.
rationale.local_fix: They proposed strncpy with null-termination but did not reject overlong input before copying. The criterion requires explicitly rejecting oversized argv prior to the copy.
rationale.global_fix: They recommended removing raw unbounded string handling by using bounded string APIs or dynamic containers. This aligns with the root-cause fix of eliminating unbounded copies from the data path.
